key_insight: Neural network depth is adapted by treating training as an optimal control problem and placing new layers where a posteriori error estimates are largest.
engineering_relevance: This gives a principled, finite-element-inspired way to build surrogate models that better capture complex nonlinear behavior with fewer wasted layers.
method: Formulate weights and biases as piecewise-linear functions over depth in a continuous-time network model.
Use dual weighted residual error estimation to compute interval-wise contributions to the total approximation error.
Insert new layers at the locations of maximum estimated error to refine architecture adaptively.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports successful tests on scientific datasets, including the observable-to-parameter map for Navier–Stokes, with improved generalization over existing adaptation methods.
watch_item: The method depends on accurate and computable error indicators, so implementation cost and estimator reliability may limit practical use on large or highly non-smooth problems.
paper_title: An optimal control approach for neural network architecture adaptation with a posteriori error estimation
